isLastMember.signature: (memberPubkeys: string[] | undefined, ownPubkeyHex: string | null | undefined) => boolean
isLastMember.invariant: Single source of truth for 'am I the last member'. Verifies own pubkey (DD-1) and fails closed on absent input (AC-ELIG-5). S3 MUST import this, not reimplement `length === 1` inline — inline drops own-pubkey verification and the fail-closed contract. DUPLICATION RISK: HIGH.

isSoleAdmin.signature: (adminPubkeys: string[] | undefined, ownPubkeyHex: string | null | undefined) => boolean
isSoleAdmin.invariant: Behavior frozen — return value MUST NOT change for any input (AC-ELIG-6).

selectLeaveModalState.invariant: Load-bearing ordering (DD-2 / AC-SELECT-1): isLastMember tested BEFORE isSoleAdmin. Fails closed: undefined members => never 'abandon' (AC-SELECT-4). Do NOT thread this call's result into leaveGroupImpl — the two live reads are independent re-derivations (DD-7 / AC-STRUCT-3).

NOT_consumed_by: S3 (leaveGroupImpl does its OWN execution-time live read — R2; do not route S3 through this method)
getLiveMemberPubkeys.invariant: Resolves undefined — never [] — on no-client/no-group/throw (AC-UX-4). Reads live MLS state fresh on every call. The marmot-ts import lives HERE not in the component (boundary rule 4 / AC-STRUCT-4).

cross_story_risks.risk: leaveGroupImpl reimplements `members.length === 1` inline instead of importing isLastMember, dropping own-pubkey verification and fail-closed semantics.
cross_story_risks.guard: Review S3 for `import { isLastMember }` from leaveEligibility; reject an inline length check.

cross_story_risks.risk: The two live-read fail-closed defaults point in OPPOSITE directions (modal -> false => blocked/confirm; impl -> false => send anyway). A future 'harmonization' into a shared helper silently breaks one.
cross_story_risks.guard: Keep the two reads as independent re-derivations; do not extract a shared 'lastMember' resolver across S2/S3.
cross_story_risks.status: S2 side HELD — component read independent, fail-closed toward blocked/confirm. Re-check when S3 impl read lands (opposite direction: default to SEND).

observations.note: Inlines client.groups.get instead of composing getGroup as the Seam table states. Behaviorally equivalent; DRY/drift only. NOT acted on — S3 does not touch this method (does its own execution-time read). Recorded as review note.

Codex flagged the abandon button still routes through leaveGroup() which sends kind-13/kind-9 before purge. CORRECT about the partial tree, but this is exactly S3's charter (AC-SEND-1): the send-skip is not in S2's scope. NOT an S2 defect — it is the not-yet-implemented next story. Do not remediate against S2. AC-SEND-1's examiner check + the final pre-commit Codex re-run on the assembled tree will confirm closure.
One 3s fixed wait gating KeyPackage/MLS init — non-load-bearing (no rendered marker to poll; assertions gate on toBeVisible). Reviewer judged defensible. Not acted on.

Per-story Codex Stage-2 for S4 could not run: node crashed with ENFILE (system-wide file-table overflow, errno -23) on two attempts — shared-host fd exhaustion, not a review result. S4 is test-only (one e2e spec, zero production-code delta since the last CLEAN Codex pass on the assembled S1+S2+S3 tree). Deferred to the mandatory pre-commit review gate, which re-runs Codex on the whole assembled tree before commit; if still ENFILE-blocked there, escalate to the user per the gate's unavailable-backend contract.

cross_story_risks_status.R1-isLastMember-reimpl: CLOSED — S3 imports isLastMember, no inline length check.
cross_story_risks_status.R2-fail-closed-unification: CLOSED — independent live read; no-mlsGroup AND getGroupMembers-throw both default to send-anyway + purge (F1 fix completes this).

codex_findings_status.O2-solo-still-sends: CLOSED by S3 — the && !lastMember gate suppresses both sends for a solo group (AC-SEND-1).
